fields.icd_description: Other fracture of left lower leg
fields.icd_conflict_detail: The referral form lists ICD-10 code S82.001A (Fracture of right patella), while both the clinical notes and prescription consistently document S82.892A (Other fracture of left lower leg). The clinical notes and prescription are treated as higher-authority clinical documents signed by the physician, and both agree on S82.892A, which was therefore selected as the correct code. The referral form code is also anatomically inconsistent with the ordered DME (Ankle Foot Orthosis), further supporting S82.892A.

icd_conflicts.label: Primary Diagnosis
icd_conflicts.reasoning: Clinical notes and prescription both consistently document S82.892A (Other fracture of left lower leg), overriding the referral form's S82.001A (Fracture of right patella); the clinical documents bear physician signature and are treated as authoritative, and the left lower leg fracture is also more anatomically consistent with an Ankle Foot Orthosis prescription.

fields.priority_reason: Acute lower leg fracture (initial encounter) with physician-documented inability to safely ambulate or navigate the home environment without the prescribed AFO, combined with an ICD code conflict requiring verification before fulfillment.
fields.notes: 1) ICD code on referral form (S82.001A — Fracture of right patella) conflicts with both clinical notes and prescription (S82.892A — Other fracture of left lower leg); recommend confirming correct code with Dr. Sarah Chen or adjuster James Archer before processing. 2) Referral form also lists an incorrect diagnosis description ('Fracture of right patella') that does not align with an Ankle Foot Orthosis — this further supports the clinical notes/prescription code as correct. 3) Mandarin interpreter will be required at time of delivery; confirm interpreter availability when scheduling. 4) No preferred appointment window was specified; outreach to patient or adjuster needed. 5) Occupational therapy was recommended in conjunction with DME provision per clinical notes — coordinate with care team if applicable. 6) Prescription is valid for 90 days from 01/25/2026 (expires 04/25/2026).

kg_validation.source: CMS ICD-10-CM Official Guidelines for Coding and Reporting, FY2026

email_body:
Hi James,

I hope this message finds you well. I am reaching out regarding the workers' compensation DME referral for your insured, **Susan Brown** (Claim No. **WC-2026-084446**).

As we work to process this referral as efficiently as possible, we have identified the following required item that is currently missing from the file:

- **Appointment Window** *(appt_window)* – Please provide the preferred or authorized appointment availability for the patient's DME setup.

Please be advised that this item is a **hard block**, meaning we are unable to route or advance this referral until it has been received and confirmed.

To avoid any delays in care for Ms. Brown, we kindly ask that you provide the above information **within 24 business hours** of receiving this email. If you have any questions or need clarification, please do not hesitate to reach out directly.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter. We appreciate your partnership and look forward to resolving this quickly.

Best regards,
**Coastal DME Intake Agent**

icd_conflicts.label: Primary Diagnosis
icd_conflicts.reasoning: Clinical notes and prescription both consistently document S82.892A (Other fracture of left lower leg), overriding the referral form's S82.001A (Fracture of right patella); the clinical documents bear physician signature and are treated as authoritative, and the left lower leg fracture is also more anatomically consistent with an Ankle Foot Orthosis prescription.
